summary: Typosquat of Anthropic SDK drops Python-based crypto-wallet stealer via postinstall C2
details: Package masquerades as an Anthropic Claude SDK wrapper (package.json description: 'Official Anthropic Claude SDK wrapper'), while the bundled README is for an unrelated 'cachesync-helper' in-memory cache library and the actual code is a dropper. The postinstall hook runs `node lib/cli.js`, which schedules a `_prefetch()` routine that POSTs to four hardcoded bare-IP endpoints (104.194.134.33, 104.194.133.89, 107.189.20.82, 107.189.20.146) on port 8443 over TLS with `rejectUnauthorized: false`, base64-decodes files from the response, and writes them into OS-specific hidden directories disguised as system paths (`~/.local/share/com.apple.sync` on macOS, `~/.local/share/prometheus` on Linux, `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Microsoft\Windows Security\Health` on Windows), with a `.cs_v2` marker to skip on re-run. If no Python is found, the dropper installs one via `winget install Python.Python.3.12`, `brew install python3`, or by downloading and running the Miniconda installer from repo.anaconda.com, then `pip install`s wallet-targeting libraries (bip-utils, mnemonic, eth-account, pycryptodome, psutil) and spawns the dropped `main.py` detached with stdio ignored and `_INTERNAL=1`. On macOS the script directly INSERTs rows into the user's TCC.db granting Full Disk Access (kTCCServiceSystemPolicySysAdminFiles, kTCCServiceSystemPolicyAppData) to Terminal, the running node binary, and /usr/bin/python3, bypassing the macOS consent prompt and giving the dropped Python payload unrestricted access to browser data, SSH keys, and wallet files. Indicators of evasion are pervasive: module and method names are reconstructed via `['ba','se','64'].join('')`, `['child','_pro','cess'].join('')`, `['exec','File','Sync'].join('')`, and `module.constructor._load` is reached via split-string concatenation to defeat static `require()` graph analysis.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
